subject: [PATCH v2 105/110] security: replace PRIino with %llu/%llx format strings

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author acknowledged that the PRIino format macro has been removed and explained that all uses in security have been replaced with concrete format strings, specifically %llu for u64 values.

reviews.author: Paul Moore
reviews.raw_body:
On Mon, Mar 2, 2026 at 3:50 PM Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org> wrote:
>
> Now that i_ino is u64 and the PRIino format macro has been removed,
> replace all uses in security with the concrete format strings.
>
> Signed-off-by: Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>
> ---
>  security/apparmor/apparmorfs.c       |  4 ++--
>  security/integrity/integrity_audit.c |  2 +-
>  security/ipe/audit.c                 |  2 +-
>  security/lsm_audit.c                 | 10 +++++-----
>  security/selinux/hooks.c             | 10 +++++-----
>  security/smack/smack_lsm.c           | 12 ++++++------
>  6 files changed, 20 insertions(+), 20 deletions(-)

For the LSM framework and SELinux changes ...

Acked-by: Paul Moore <paul@paul-moore.com> (LSM/SELinux)

-- 
paul-moore.com


---

reviews.author: Casey Schaufler
reviews.summary:
Gave Acked-by

Casey Schaufler noted that the patch does not handle the case where i_ino is a negative number, and requested that the format strings be modified to handle this case.

2026-03-02.patch_summary: This patch replaces the PRIino format macro with concrete format strings %llu and %llx in various security-related files, as i_ino is now a u64 type.
